summary:
Added acquire/release synchronization between producer and consumer so slot data is visible before indices advance, fixing the memory ordering bug.

- ring_buffer.c:4-9 rewrote the memory-ordering rationale to describe the release/acquire scheme that now backs the implementation.
- ring_buffer.c:79-120 switched the producer’s read_idx load to `memory_order_acquire`, the write_idx publication to `memory_order_release`, mirrored the consumer side, and aligned the inline comments with the new semantics.
- ring_buffer.h:28-38 updated the API documentation to spell out the release/acquire coordination between the two atomics.

Tests:
- `./test.sh` (PASS)

Next step: 1) Optionally re-run on the deployment platform (with TSan if available) to confirm the fix under its actual runtime environment.
